---
title: "Glossary"
---

Key terms used across the book. Each links back to the chapter where it first appears.


### capital per effective worker _(also: k-tilde)_

Capital divided by labor measured in efficiency units, k̃ = K/(AL). The variable that converges in Solow. — [↪ first used in *The Solow Growth Model*](content/solow_intro.html)

### chain-weighting

Updating price weights every period and chaining the growth rates — the compromise between base-year choices used in official statistics. — [↪ first used in *Real GDP with Changing Prices*](content/two_goods_twoperiods.html)

### consumption smoothing

The tendency of forward-looking agents to even out consumption across periods rather than match it to income timing. — [↪ first used in *Two-Period Consumption*](content/two_period_consumption.html)

### depreciation

The fraction of the capital stock that wears out each period, denoted δ. — [↪ first used in *The Solow Growth Model*](content/solow_intro.html)

### diminishing returns

Each additional unit of capital adds less output than the last — the force that pulls the economy to a steady state. — [↪ first used in *Cobb-Douglas Production*](content/cobb_d_prod.html)

### Euler equation

The optimality condition for consumption over time: marginal utility today equals (1+r)·β·marginal utility tomorrow. Here: c₂ = β(1+r)c₁. — [↪ first used in *Two-Period Consumption*](content/two_period_consumption.html)

### expectations-augmented Phillips curve _(also: AS curve)_

Inflation equals expected inflation plus a term in the output gap: π = πᵉ + λ(Y − Ȳ). — [↪ first used in *Dynamic AD-AS with a Taylor Rule*](content/adassim.html)

### expenditure approach

Measuring GDP as total final spending: Y = C + I + G + NX. — [↪ first used in *Measuring GDP: The Expenditure Approach*](content/measuring_gdp.html)

### GDP deflator

The ratio of nominal to real GDP (×100) — the economy-wide price index implied by the accounts. — [↪ first used in *Real vs. Nominal GDP*](content/realvsnom.html)

### intertemporal budget constraint _(also: IBC)_

All (c₁,c₂) bundles whose present value equals lifetime wealth: c₁ + c₂/(1+r) = W. — [↪ first used in *Two-Period Consumption*](content/two_period_consumption.html)

### nominal GDP

Output valued at current prices — price and quantity changes mixed together. — [↪ first used in *Real vs. Nominal GDP*](content/realvsnom.html)

### output gap

The deviation of output from potential, Y − Ȳ. Positive gaps push inflation up via the Phillips curve. — [↪ first used in *Dynamic AD-AS with a Taylor Rule*](content/adassim.html)

### purchasing power parity _(also: PPP)_

Valuing every country's output at one common set of prices, so cross-country comparisons reflect quantities. — [↪ first used in *Comparing GDP Across Countries*](content/realgdpacross.html)

### real GDP

Output valued at constant prices, so changes reflect quantities rather than prices. — [↪ first used in *Real vs. Nominal GDP*](content/realvsnom.html)

### savings rate _(also: s)_

The constant fraction s of output that households save and firms invest each period. — [↪ first used in *The Solow Growth Model*](content/solow_intro.html)

### steady state _(also: k*, balanced growth path)_

The level where a variable stops changing over time. In Solow, where investment exactly offsets depreciation + dilution, so k̇ = 0. — [↪ first used in *The Solow Growth Model*](content/solow_intro.html)

### Taylor principle

For stability the policy response must be more than one-for-one with inflation (φπ > 1); otherwise inflation gaps are self-reinforcing. — [↪ first used in *Dynamic AD-AS with a Taylor Rule*](content/adassim.html)

### Taylor rule

A monetary-policy rule: the central bank raises the real rate above neutral when inflation exceeds target — rₜ = r̄ + φπ(πₜ₋₁ − π*). — [↪ first used in *Dynamic AD-AS with a Taylor Rule*](content/adassim.html)

### total factor productivity _(also: TFP, A)_

The efficiency term A multiplying the production function; its growth rate g sets long-run per-capita growth. — [↪ first used in *The Solow Growth Model*](content/solow_intro.html)
